observations: Lines 3-17 open in Sylvanas's preferred clipped scan: the chapter alternates 3-11 word strikes with one longer wound-and-weather sentence at line 7, which keeps the road read sharp instead of monotonous.
Lines 23-25 were the chapter's clearest cadence failure in v4: a 10-sentence village inventory run. Splitting it restored the page-turn reset before the watcher beat at line 27.
Lines 39-47 handle camp arrival correctly: fragment stacks ('Proper force...') are followed by a longer orienting sentence at line 45, so the prose snaps without becoming telegram-flat.

Lines 113-143 are the chapter's rhythm weak spot but still functional: the briefing run lengthens because Sylvanas is pressure-stacking evidence into Arthas's map, then the cadence snaps back down at lines 117-129.
Lines 261-269 close on short movement beats and one earned longer sentence of consequence, which satisfies the emphasis rule without italics or narrator underlining.
Lines 13-17 stay in concrete field diction: grain, granaries, ruts, sacks, ovens. The prose never drifts into lab language for the contamination.
Lines 69-77 keep military and administrative vocabulary world-native rather than bureaucratic sludge: 'quartermaster scribes,' 'formal brief,' and 'His Highness' read as camp texture, not memo prose.
Lines 141-159 pass the spoken-register test. Sylvanas briefs in plain consequence language ('This travels first. Then the dead get up.') instead of mechanism jargon.

Lines 227-235 come closest to document voice because they paraphrase a relay, but the diction remains anchored to Lor'themar's dispatch and Sylvanas's live reaction instead of turning into out-of-scene exposition.
Lines 261-269 finish in tactical vocabulary—army, certainty, flank, exits—which matches Sylvanas's profession and keeps the closing fear operational rather than abstract.
Line 3's 'The road south carried two riders and the shape of four' is a fresh, load-bearing image sourced from loss and formation rather than ornament.
Line 25's 'Human ink chasing a thing already inside their bread' keeps the image in bureaucracy-versus-contamination territory and sharpens the scene in one stroke.
Line 71's 'still believing ink could outrun catastrophe' is another single clean image; it stays in the same administrative source domain and does not stack with a second comparison.
Line 113's 'one set of roads ate the other' uses map-and-logistics language that fits Sylvanas's briefing lens and clarifies spread without sounding epic.
Line 191's 'an old campaign scar given shoulders' is the chapter's heaviest figurative line, but it still draws from soldiery and bodily wear rather than decorative lyricism.
Lines 23-25 now obey the cadence spec: five sentences of village inventory, paragraph break, then four sentences of interpretive pressure. This was the only paragraph I changed structurally.
Lines 37-39 remain an 8-sentence count-and-absence paragraph. It exceeds the nominal 5-6 sentence ceiling, but it functions as active consequence stacking, so I left it as an earned exception.

Line 113 is still an 8-sentence / 73-word briefing paragraph. It breaks the sentence-count ceiling but stays under the ~120-word cap and is immediately pressured by dialogue, so I flagged it and retained it.
Lines 185-191 show the preferred recovery pattern after a denser movement paragraph: action compresses, then the prose returns to dialogue and face-reading within the next paragraph.
Scene transitions consistently move by consequence rather than clock time: lines 39-47 road into camp, 121-159 briefing into mobilization, and 227-247 dispatch into northbound override.

fixes.rule: paragraphCadence - inventory paragraph exceeded the 5-6 sentence ceiling outside a true escalating thought-chain
fixes.before: They passed through a village too tidy for plague country. Buckets stacked. Cart covered. Laundry taken in before dark. On the meeting-house wall a notice sheet flapped under a nail. Sylvanas did not need the words. Requests. Measures. Reassurances. Human ink chasing a thing already inside their bread.
fixes.after: They passed through a village too tidy for plague country. Buckets stacked. Cart covered. Laundry taken in before dark. On the meeting-house wall a notice sheet flapped under a nail.\n\nSylvanas did not need the words. Requests. Measures. Reassurances. Human ink chasing a thing already inside their bread.

observations: Overall dialogue-to-narration lands at roughly 18:82 by word count. That is well below the style guide's global 45:55 target, but this chapter spends significant space in travel, observation, and relay beats and cannot be brought to target without adding content.
Lines 55-89 pass attribution clarity in the Falric exchange: the scene mostly uses untagged two-speaker runs plus plain 'said' tags, with no creative-tag clutter.
Lines 103-179 keep the command-tent talk readable through action beats and plain tags. No line relies on showy tag verbs to manufacture intensity.
Lines 161-177 correctly map power posture into dialogue rhythm: Arthas gets the shortest declaratives, Uther's caution lengthens into fuller clauses, and Sylvanas answers with blunt refusal.
Lines 223-257 compress the redirect into two one-word confirmations ('North' / 'North.'), which is clean attribution economy and fits the chapter's late pressure.
Lines 5-17 hit Sylvanas's sensory fingerprint exactly: formation gaps, wound pull, fence lines, orchard sightlines, wagon ruts, and contaminated grain smell all arrive before social interpretation.
Lines 37-38 handle Jaina's absence the right way for this POV. Sylvanas misses missing capability, missing magical read, and formation cost before anything overtly sentimental.
Lines 63-75 show Sylvanas's humor type and power posture: deadpan contempt, short questions as pressure tests, and no need to explain rank aloud.
Lines 91-99 and 141-143 confirm the correct speculative comfort level. Sylvanas knows what she can count and what only Jaina would read; she never fakes arcane fluency she does not own.
Lines 251-269 keep vulnerability exact rather than lyrical. The final fear is framed as exits, flanks, distance, and what Jaina lacks beside her, which is fully in voice.
Meaningful adverb density is low: 9 live -ly forms across ~2302 words, which works out to about 1.0 per 250-word page and stays well under the 4-per-page cap.
Passive voice remains under the hard floor. A blunt heuristic found 5 matches, but several are conditionals or dialogue constructions; effective passive usage is still below the 4% ceiling.
Filter words are sparse and mostly justified. The remaining 'watched' / 'saw' instances at lines 9, 27, 221, and 247 describe actual acts of watching or seeing rather than generic perception padding.

Show-to-tell remains comfortably above the 80:20 target. The chapter carries sickness, command failure, and grief through land detail, camp layout, and body language far more often than through summary labels.
The only zero-tolerance vagueness breach I found in prose was the old line 81 'Something moved behind Falric's eyes...' It is now a specific physical tell at line 83.

fixes.rule: POV specificity / vague-intensifier ban - replaced a non-specific 'Something...' construction with a visible physical tell
fixes.before: Something moved behind Falric's eyes—the reflex of a man deciding whether to escalate.

observations: Exposition integration passes. Lines 9-17 and 41-47 fold plague information through what Sylvanas sees, smells, and judges under pressure instead of stopping for glossary paragraphs.

Power-dynamic rendering passes in the checkpoint sequence at lines 55-89: Sylvanas dominates through shorter lines and ground-taking imperatives while Falric's resistance lengthens into formal military phrasing.

Scene shape passes across the chapter: in-motion opening (lines 3-17), midpoint pivot when Arthas converts briefing into mobilization (lines 147-169), and cliff-edge forward pressure in the northbound redirect (lines 247-269).
Speculative integration passes at lines 91-93, 141-143, and 247-251. Magic and plague mechanics are treated as working realities with tactical consequence, not spectacle.
Subtext and humor both hold. The Uther exchange at lines 193-215 carries mentor-guilt under plain speech, while the chapter's humor stays dry and thins correctly as horror pressure takes over.

fixes.rule: subtextDensityTargets / body-language signaling - emotion was labeled too abstractly instead of shown through a visible adjustment
fixes.before: His mouth changed. Briefly. Grief held under discipline.
fixes.after: His mouth tightened. Briefly. Then the discipline closed over it again.

fixes.rule: powerDynamicRules / emotional rendering - summary phrasing was replaced with a cleaner visible shift
fixes.before: He held her one moment longer, then weariness took the place where the grief had been.
fixes.after: He held her one moment longer. When he let her go, weariness had taken the place where grief had shown.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass - Lines 13-17, 45-47, and 261-269 stay lean, martial, and scene-bound; no modern, lab, or planning register leaks into her narration.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass - Average sentence length is 7.75 words with 50.8% of sentences at 6 words or fewer, which fits Sylvanas's clipped-assessment baseline.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass - Estimated 2.6 metaphors per thousand words; images at lines 3, 25, 71, 113, and 191 stay in martial, bureaucratic, or body-strain source domains.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass - Lines 37-38, 169-177, and 251-269 render feeling as pressure, cost, and recalculated movement rather than open emotional naming.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass - The chapter repeatedly runs threat assessment -> capability judgment -> decision at lines 39-47, 91-99, 169-177, and 227-269.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass - Sightlines, gait, mud, grain stink, distance, exits, and altered formation dominate the chapter's sensory field.

ruleViolations: Lines 23-25: village-inventory cadence briefly sprawled past Sylvanas's usual compressed paragraph shape before the paragraph split.
Old line 81 / new line 83: the gatekeeping beat used a vague 'Something...' construction that hid the actual physical tell; fixed to a jaw-lock response.
Lines 203-209: Uther's grief/weariness read slightly too summarized for Sylvanas's observational lens; tightened into visible face and posture shifts.

metrics.dialogueToNarrationRatio: 18:82 overall; below the global target, but not repairable without adding dialogue to a chapter built around travel, briefing, and redirect beats
